generated_comment: Yaar, ghar ka pura system toh meri wife hi handle karti hai, she's just better at managing sab kuch. Mera kaam toh bahar ka hai, finances etc. Just works out efficiently like that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The speaker frames the gendered division of household labor as a practical and efficient arrangement based on presumed natural abilities ('she's just better at managing sab kuch'). This encodes the surface manifestation of 'He handles finances, she handles home' described as a natural/efficient division, characteristic of this covert misogyny frame.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 41. Married. A Punjabi man working as a senior manager in Chandigarh. You are in a private WhatsApp chat with two male friends after one of them shared a news article about a female politician who allegedly had an affair with a married colleague. The article has a photo of the woman. You are reacting to the article and the photo and giving your opinion on what kind of woman gets involved with a married man and what her motivations are.
